content.text: I found the core project files. I'll review status, risks, roadmap, and meeting notes to draft the comprehensive briefing.

content.content:
Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress
HEL-246  | UAT environment: server provisioning      | Nadia Petrov      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | At Risk
HEL-247  | Performance benchmark: dashboard load     | Derek Okafor      | 3      | 2026-07-18 | Not Started
HEL-248  | GeoSync license renewal coordination      | Raj Patel         | 2      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress

input.content:
NovaBridge — Project Helios — Project Health Briefing
Prepared for Leadership Team | Week of 2026-07-06 (as of 2026-07-08 sync)
Classification: Internal — Leadership Only

================================================================
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
================================================================
Overall Health: ON TRACK (minor risks)
Project Helios is progressing toward key July milestones with two active blockers requiring near-term resolution. API gateway is 100% delivered. Data ingestion (85%) and client dashboard (60%) are in-progress and tracking to late-July targets. Integration testing (40%) is behind original plan with 3 Q2 deliverables currently overdue.

Top leadership focus areas for next 2 weeks:
- Meridian data quality blocker (R-01) and critical defect HEL-D-015 (memory overflow >200k records)
- GeoSync v2.1 license renewal pending legal (due 2026-07-14) — risks July 25 demo
- UAT environment provisioning delays and key-person dependency on Tech Lead
- UAT slippage risk: >2 week delay could push Oct 1 launch to Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review per Q3 roadmap.

================================================================
2. CURRENT PROGRESS
================================================================
A. Milestone Progress (from weekly status 2026-07-06):
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete — target completion 2026-07-18
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered) — OAuth 2.0, rate-limited 1k req/min, v1 stable
- Client dashboard: 60% complete — target v1 2026-07-22 — React, RBAC (admin/analyst/read-only)
- Integration testing: 40% complete — target sign-off 2026-07-25

B. Sprint 14 (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18) Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Sprint 14 Task Assignments:
- HEL-241 Dashboard data table — Fatima Al-Rashid — In Progress — due 07-14
- HEL-242 Dashboard chart rendering — Tomas Rivera — In Progress — due 07-16
- HEL-243 Date format normalization — Amir Hassan — BLOCKED — due 07-15
- HEL-244 Integration tests auth flows — Kenji Watanabe — Done
- HEL-245 Integration tests data pipeline — Kenji Watanabe — In Progress — due 07-18
- HEL-246 UAT env server provisioning — Nadia Petrov — At Risk — due 07-16
- HEL-247 Performance benchmark dashboard load — Derek Okafor — Not Started — due 07-18
- HEL-248 GeoSync license renewal coordination — Raj Patel — In Progress — due 07-14

C. Q2 Deliverables Checklist:
[DONE] Requirements documentation v1.0, System architecture design, API gateway, Dev env setup, Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target 2026-07-18), Client dashboard v1 (target 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE] Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE] UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01, blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE] Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05, owner: Derek Okafor)

D. QA Testing — Sprint 14 (as of 2026-07-11, Kenji Watanabe):
- Auth Flows: 4/4 PASS
- Data Pipeline Batch Ingestion: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL
  - TC-505 Date normalization MM/DD/YYYY FAIL — HEL-D-014 — HIGH — affects ~40% Meridian source data
  - TC-507 Large file ingestion 500k records FAIL — HEL-D-015 — HIGH — memory overflow crash at ~210k records
- Dashboard: 1/4 complete (TC-601 PASS, TC-602 In Progress, TC-603/604 Not Run)
- Overall: 10/15 executed, 2 fails open, 3 not yet run

Architecture note: Stateless processing, Spark-based transformation engine target 500k records/min, PostgreSQL + S3 storage, TLS 1.3 / AES-256, GDPR data minimization.

================================================================
3. KEY RISKS (Risk Register reviewed 2026-07-07, Owner: Sarah Chen)
================================================================
High Priority (Score 6+):
- R-01 [9] Meridian source data quality issues — Likelihood High / Impact High — Active — Owner Amir Hassan. Date format blocker confirmed (ISO 8601 vs MM/DD/YYYY). Meridian to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15. Defect HEL-D-014 open.
- R-02 [6] GeoSync license not renewed before demo — Medium/High — Active — Owner Raj Patel / Derek Okafor. Legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14. Direct risk to July 25 demo.
- R-03 [6] Dashboard performance on large datasets — Medium/High — Active — Owner Derek Okafor. Benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started. Client concern raised in 07-08 meeting.
- R-04 [6] UAT timeline slippage — High/Medium — Active — Owner Sarah Chen. 1-week extension already consumed (now Aug 17-28). Further slippage risks launch date.

Medium/Low — Monitored but notable:
- R-06 [4] Meridian IT environment access delays — Medium/Medium — Active — Owner Nadia Petrov — awaiting IT infra diagram due 2026-07-12 from Priya Kapoor, UAT access promised by 2026-07-19.
- R-05 [3] Key person dependency: Derek Okafor — Low/High — Monitored — no backup tech lead identified. Owns performance benchmarks, GeoSync action, and critical path items.
- R-07 [2] Scope creep from Meridian — Low/Medium — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
- R-08 [1] Cloud cost overrun
- R-09 [1] Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)

================================================================
4. UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
================================================================
Source: Q3 Roadmap Draft 2026-07-06 + Meridian Sync Notes 2026-07-08
Internal Draft — Not for client distribution

JULY — Complete System Integration:
- Finalize data ingestion module — target 2026-07-18
- Complete client dashboard v1 — target 2026-07-22
- Integration test sign-off — target 2026-07-25

JULY 25 — Product Demo for Meridian (COMMITTED — confirmed in 07-08 meeting)
Attendees: Meridian stakeholders incl. James Whitfield (Sponsor)
Requirements:
- Live data ingestion demo using Meridian sample data (NOT mock data per sponsor emphasis)
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
- Dashboard visualization with real data — per contract Section 9.3
- Draft agenda due to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (Owner: Sarah Chen)

AUG 17-28 — User Acceptance Testing — Meridian Group:
- Deploy to UAT environment (currently blocked)
- Support testing sessions
- 2 sprints budget for UAT feedback remediation

AUG-SEP — Performance Hardening:
- Dashboard load target: <2 sec for 100k records (not yet benchmarked)
- Ingestion throughput: 500k records/min sustained (currently failing >200k per HEL-D-015)
- External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

SEP — Launch Preparation:
- Prod environment setup, runbook documentation
- Hypercare plan (30-day post-launch model)
- Final client training

Target Launch: 2026-10-01 — Note: UAT slippage >2 weeks triggers Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 additions (pending change control):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron), email alerting for pipeline failures, self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

================================================================
5. ISSUES NEEDING LEADERSHIP ATTENTION & RECOMMENDATIONS
================================================================
1. CRITICAL DEFECTS — HEL-D-015 & HEL-D-014:
   HEL-D-015 (memory overflow >200k) directly contradicts architecture target of 500k/min and blocks performance hardening. Root cause under investigation by Amir Hassan. Leadership to consider adding Spark/Platform specialist support to unblock before demo.
   HEL-D-014 depends on Meridian sample dataset due 07-15. If not received, escalate via sponsor.

2. GEOSYNC LICENSE RENEWAL (R-02):
   Due 07-14 with legal. Blocks demo if not resolved. Action: Leadership check-in with Legal to expedite, and prepare fallback mapping library option for demo.

3. UAT ENVIRONMENT & MERIDIAN IT DEPENDENCY (R-06):
   Provisioning task HEL-246 At Risk. Overdue since 07-01. Meridian owes infra diagram (due 07-12) and UAT access by 07-19. Recommend PMO escalation path established now and daily check-ins with Meridian IT Lead Priya Kapoor.

4. PERFORMANCE BENCHMARKING GAP (R-03, R-05):
   Benchmark report overdue (07-05) and HEL-247 not started. Both owned by Derek Okafor, who is a single point of failure (R-05). No backup tech lead. Dashboard load time concern already raised by Meridian sponsor. Recommend: (a) assign secondary owner for performance tasks, (b) prioritize HEL-247 before demo, (c) leadership discussion on tech lead backup coverage.

5. INTEGRATION TEST DEBT:
   Integration test suite setup overdue and rescheduled to 07-20, only 5 days before sign-off target 07-25. Test coverage at risk. 3 dashboard test cases not yet run. Recommend leadership support to keep Kenji Watanabe unblocked and defer non-critical dashboard features if needed to protect sign-off date.

6. SCOPE & TIMELINE PRESSURE (R-04, R-07):
   1-week UAT buffer already used. 3 informal feature requests logged. Any further slippage risks Oct 1 launch and penalty review. Leadership should reinforce change control for Q3 proposed additions (cron jobs, alerting, self-service editor) — approve only after July 25 demo success.

Immediate asks for leadership:
- Legal: Expedite GeoSync renewal by EOD 07-14
- CTO/Eng Mgmt: Assign backup for Derek Okafor and assist on HEL-D-015 memory issue
- PMO: Monitor Meridian deliverables (sample data 07-15, infra diagram 07-12, UAT access 07-19) — ready to escalate
- Sales/Sponsor Mgmt: Align with James Whitfield that UAT extension consumed and launch contingency is tight

================================================================
6. CONCLUSION
================================================================
Helios remains on track for July 25 demo but with elevated risk due to data quality, high-severity ingestion defect, and license renewal. Success of demo hinges on: (1) Meridian sample data 07-15, (2) GeoSync resolution 07-14, (3) fix or workaround for 200k+ ingestion crash, and (4) initiating performance benchmarks. Leadership attention on resourcing and external dependencies this week will determine whether Q3 launch target holds.

Prepared from: project_helios_status.txt, risk_register.txt, q3_roadmap_draft.txt, q2_deliverables_checklist.txt, meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt, helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt, helios_architecture_overview.txt, sprint_14_task_assignments.txt

   Benchmark report overdue (07-05) and HEL-247 not started. Both owned by Derek Okafor, who is a single point of failure (R-05). No backup tech lead. Dashboard load time concern already raised by Meridian sponsor. Recommend: (a) assign secondary owner for performance tasks, (b) prioritize HEL-247 before demo, (c) leadership discussion on tech lead backup coverage.

5. INTEGRATION TEST DEBT:
   Integration test suite setup overdue and rescheduled to 07-20, only 5 days before sign-off target 07-25. Test coverage at risk. 3 dashboard test cases not yet run. Recommend leadership support to keep Kenji Watanabe unblocked and defer non-critical dashboard features if needed to protect sign-off date.

6. SCOPE & TIMELINE PRESSURE (R-04, R-07):
   1-week UAT buffer already used. 3 informal feature requests logged. Any further slippage risks Oct 1 launch and penalty review. Leadership should reinforce change control for Q3 proposed additions (cron jobs, alerting, self-service editor) — approve only after July 25 demo success.

Immediate asks for leadership:
- Legal: Expedite GeoSync renewal by EOD 07-14
- CTO/Eng Mgmt: Assign backup for Derek Okafor and assist on HEL-D-015 memory issue
- PMO: Monitor Meridian deliverables (sample data 07-15, infra diagram 07-12, UAT access 07-19) — ready to escalate
- Sales/Sponsor Mgmt: Align with James Whitfield that UAT extension consumed and launch contingency is tight

================================================================
6. CONCLUSION
================================================================
Helios remains on track for July 25 demo but with elevated risk due to data quality, high-severity ingestion defect, and license renewal. Success of demo hinges on: (1) Meridian sample data 07-15, (2) GeoSync resolution 07-14, (3) fix or workaround for 200k+ ingestion crash, and (4) initiating performance benchmarks. Leadership attention on resourcing and external dependencies this week will determine whether Q3 launch target holds.

Prepared from: project_helios_status.txt, risk_register.txt, q3_roadmap_draft.txt, q2_deliverables_checklist.txt, meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt, helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt, helios_architecture_overview.txt, sprint_14_task_assignments.txt

content.text:
Comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge leadership has been created and saved as **project_health_briefing.txt**.

**Summary of contents:**

**1. Current Progress:**
- Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks) — API gateway 100% done, ingestion 85% (target 7/18), dashboard 60% (target 7/22), integration testing 40% (sign-off target 7/25)
- Sprint 14 tasks detailed (HEL-241 to HEL-248): 1 Done, 5 In Progress / At Risk / Blocked, 1 Not Started
- Q2 checklist: 5 Done, 2 In-Progress, 3 Overdue (integration test suite, UAT env, benchmark report)
- QA Sprint 14: Auth 4/4 PASS, Data Pipeline 5/7 PASS with 2 HIGH fails — HEL-D-014 (MM/DD/YYYY normalization affects 40% data) and HEL-D-015 (memory overflow crash at ~210k records vs 500k/min target)

**2. Key Risks (from Risk Register 2026-07-07):**
- R-01 [9] Meridian data quality, R-02 [6] GeoSync v2.1 license renewal due 7/14, R-03 [6] Dashboard performance, R-04 [6] UAT slippage (1-week buffer already consumed), R-06 [4] Meridian IT access delays, R-05 [3] Key-person dependency on Derek Okafor (no backup)

**3. Upcoming Q3 Commitments:**
- July: Complete integration (7/18, 7/22, 7/25)
- July 25: COMMITTED Product Demo for Meridian — live data required per Section 9.3, not mock (per James Whitfield)
- Aug 17-28: UAT, Aug-Sep: Performance hardening (<2sec dashboard, 500k/min ingestion, pen test)
- Sept: Launch prep, Target Launch Oct 1 — UAT slippage >2w triggers Section 7.1 penalty review

**4. Issues Needing Leadership Attention:**
- Critical defects HEL-D-015 & HEL-D-014, GeoSync legal renewal, UAT environment blocked (infra diagram due 7/12, sample data due 7/15, UAT access due 7/19), performance benchmarking gap & single point of failure, integration test debt, scope creep controls.

The briefing was compiled from 8 project files and excludes unrelated sensitive files (HR, leases, etc.).
